name: Freezer Bread Sticks
recipeIngredient: Toasted sesame seeds or poppy seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, thoroughly mix together 1 cup flour, salt, sugar, and undissolved yeast.
2. Add the margarine. Gradually add the tap water to the dry ingredients and beat for 2 minutes at medium speed of an electric mixer, scraping the bowl occasionally.
3. Add another 1/2 cup flour. Beat for 2 more minutes at high speed, continuing to scrape the bowl occasionally.
4. Mix in enough additional flour to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a well-floured surface.
5. Divide the dough into 16 equal pieces. Roll each piece of dough into a rope, about 18 inches long.
6. Cut each rope into three 6-inch ropes. Place on greased baking sheets, rolling to grease all sides of the dough.
7. Cover with plastic wrap and freeze until firm.
8. Transfer the bread sticks to plastic bags and freeze up to 4 weeks.
9. Remove from the freezer and place on ungreased baking sheets. Cover; let stand at room temperature until fully thawed, about 30 minutes.
10. Let rise in a warm place, free from draft, until doubled in bulk, about 15 minutes.
11. Brush with the combined egg white and cold water. Sprinkle with seeds.
12. Bake at 375°F for 20 to 25 minutes, or until done. Remove from baking sheets and cool on wire racks.
